title: Large volume — R3 close
description: Large 200k-unit cotton order at moderate budget (₹420). Demonstrates that quantity scales the working-capital math the treasury sub-agent runs (ACTUS PAM cashflow simulation) — at this volume NPV is healthy and treasury approves, so the deal closes inside the same R3-accept band as smaller orders. Useful for showing that volume alone does not change the closing price band in today's product; that is by design for FRAMEWORK-V2 §3.1 (mode determines reasoning depth, not quantity).

expectedOutcome.likely: 3-round close at ~₹377–₹395/unit. Total deal ~₹75.4–₹79M — the largest deal in the scenario set. Demonstrates the L2 advisor pipeline scaling cleanly to bulk volume.
expectedOutcome.possible: Treasury cashflow horizon (Net-30 invoice inflow vs IED production outflow) may briefly flag a working-capital constraint at this scale; expected to clear because ₹350 cost × 200k = ₹70M outflow vs ~₹76M revenue inflow keeps NPV positive.
expectedOutcome.failureMode: If the seller's inventory advisor fixture reports stock < 200000, the L2 path will surface a defensive action in the audit. The fixture (erpnext-bin-FAB-COTTON-180GSM.json) determines this — check it if you want to predict the demo's exact behavior.

declaredButDeferred: sellerIntent (entirely — seller acts on its own .env config)
